message:
feat(examples): add JoinSet, gen_server, and pipe I/O examples

Three new examples demonstrating recently-added modules:

- ex_join_set.c: Dynamic task collection with spawn, poll_next
  completion iteration, and is_empty check
- ex_gen_server.c: OTP-style counter server with named start,
  call (sync request/reply), cast (async), and graceful stop
- ex_pipe_io.c: Anonymous pipe creation, buffered write/read,
  data integrity verification, and disconnection detection

All examples emit SCENARIO lines for e2e harness integration.
15 total examples now (up from 12).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-66l.8","title":"Implement per-target binary-size and cold-start SLO gates","description":"Background:
Idea round 2 includes per-target binary size and cold-start gates as embedded adoption multipliers.

Subtasks:
- Capture size and cold-start baselines for key target profiles and triplets.
- Define budget policy and tolerance windows per profile/resource class.
- Integrate checks into CI with clear delta reporting and reproducible rerun commands.
- Emit structured gate artifacts (target, baseline, delta, suspect modules, run metadata) for trend analysis and release review.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regressions in size/start metrics fail gate when outside approved budgets.
- Reports identify changed targets and likely contributing modules.
- Baseline updates require explicit rationale and audit trail.
- Gate artifacts are machine-readable and linkable from release evidence bundles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.423164995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:29:18.543969085Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:29:18.543951361Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","embedded","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.9","title":"Enforce per-bead evidence linkage contract for unit/e2e/logging completeness","description":"Background:
The hard-part verification rules require every semantic-sensitive implementation bead to carry explicit proof linkage, not only CI-level gating.

Subtasks:
- Define required evidence fields per implementation bead: linked unit tests, linked e2e scripts, expected artifact manifests, and replay/parity references.
- Require linkage against the unit/invariant completeness matrix (bd-1md.12) and canonical e2e suite contract (bd-1md.10) so evidence remains coverage-complete, not ad hoc.
- Add validation tooling that checks bead metadata and notes for required evidence references before status transitions to done.
- Integrate checks with anti-butchering proof-block gate and traceability index updates.
- Provide reviewer/operator guidance for interpreting evidence packs quickly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive beads cannot be marked complete without linked unit/e2e/logging evidence.
- Evidence packs reference explicit unit/invariant obligations and at least one canonical e2e lane per covered semantic surface.
- Validation failures are explicit and actionable.
- Evidence links remain machine-checkable for audit and release  {"id":"bd-aw69","title":"EPIC: Embedded Target Matrix Gate (GATE-EMBEDDED)","description":"## Goal
Implement GATE-EMBEDDED: cross-compilation for ARM and RISC-V targets with QEMU-based smoke
tests, validating that the C port builds and runs correctly on constrained embedded platforms.

## Why This Matters
The C port's raison d'être is portability to embedded targets that Rust's async runtime cannot
reach (bare-metal MCUs, router-class devices, automotive ECUs). Without actually building and
testing on these targets, the "portable ANSI C" claim is aspirational rather than proven.

GATE-EMBEDDED is one of the 9 non-negotiable quality gates from AGENTS.md:
"Embedded target matrix gate (cross-build + QEMU + device smoke)."

The three resource classes (R1=tight, R2=balanced, R3=roomy) defined in asx_config.h are
specifically designed for embedded deployment. The FREESTANDING and EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles
compile with no libc dependency. This gate proves those profiles work end-to-end.

## What "Done" Looks Like
- Cross-compilation succeeds for: arm-none-eabi (Cortex-M), aarch64-linux-gnu, riscv32-unknown-elf
- QEMU user-mode runs the C test suite for aarch64 and riscv32 targets
- Bare-metal builds (arm-none-eabi) link successfully with FREESTANDING profile
- Resource class R1 builds fit within 64KB RAM budget
- make embedded-matrix passes in CI

## Architectural Context
The Makefile already has profile-parity targets that build with ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING and
ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER. The freestanding hooks (src/platform/freestanding/hooks.c) provide
stub implementations for platforms without libc. What's missing is the actual cross-compiler
toolchains, QEMU setup, and the CI integration to exercise them.

## Risk
- Cross-compiler toolchain installation varies by host OS
- QEMU user-mode may not perfectly emulate all target quirks
- Bare-metal linking requires a linker script and startup code
- R1 memory budget (64KB) may require tuning ASX_MAX_* constants","notes":"## CRITICAL: EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE AUDIT (2026-03-12)

The following ALREADY EXISTS and must NOT be recreated or modified:
- tools/ci/run_embedded_matrix.sh: Linux-musl cross-build matrix (armv7, aarch64, mipsel)
- tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh: QEMU testing for Linux-musl targets
- make ci-embedded-matrix: Makefile target for Linux-musl embedded builds
- tools/ci/generate_layout_budget_report.sh: Binary size/layout reporting
- tools/ci/compute_layout_budget_delta.sh: Cross-run regression detection

## What This Epic ACTUALLY Adds (COMPLEMENTARY, NOT REPLACEMENT)
Bare-metal cross-compilation for FREESTANDING/EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles on:
- ARM Cortex-M (arm-none-eabi-gcc) — new toolchain
- RISC-V (riscv-elf-gcc) — new toolchain
- QEMU semihosting for bare-metal binaries — new execution mode
- R1 memory budgets for .text/.bss sections — new measurements

## Subtask Inventory (8 total)
.1 Install bare-metal ARM toolchain (60m) — Linux-musl ARM already exists
.2 Install RISC-V toolchain (60m) — genuinely new
.3 QEMU semihosting setup (90m) — Linux user-mode QEMU already exists
.4 Bare-metal Makefile targets (120m) — Linux-musl targets already exist
.5 Bare-metal link validation (120m) — genuinely new
.6 R1 memory budgets (90m) — layout reporting exists for musl
.7 CI gate for bare-metal (120m) — embedded CI exists for musl
.8 QEMU e2e tests for bare-metal (180m) — QEMU smoke exists for musl","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":840,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:25.215072286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:35.547567356Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:35.546118414Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","phase3","portability"]}
